scope: Learner-owned tutor thread list/read/archive/reopen, per-message provider provenance, server-authoritative append denial for archived threads, export/privacy projection, and responsive TutorView integration.

controls.ownership: All list/read/mutation queries bind chat_thread.user_id to the authenticated session user. Missing and cross-owner thread identifiers share the same 404 projection.
controls.statusDomain: Learner history exposes only active and archived threads; deleted rows are excluded. Archived content remains readable but cannot be appended until an optimistic, server-owned reopen succeeds.
controls.concurrency: Archive/reopen uses SELECT FOR UPDATE and expected updated_at. Exact target-state retries are idempotent. Tutor persistence repeats an active-status owner predicate inside its transaction before inserting chat messages.
controls.privacy: No browser storage is used. UI and export expose only provider, model, prompt version, and learner/admin-fallback source; credential IDs and key material remain excluded.
controls.pagination: Thread and message history use bounded keyset cursors with strict UUID/timestamp decoding and maximum cursor length.

verification.command: npx.cmd vitest run <7 focused chat/route/component/export files>

verification.database: disposable PostgreSQL 17 container; migrations applied twice

verification.command: npm.cmd run lint && npm.cmd run typecheck && npm.cmd run security:secrets

remainingExternalEvidence: No live external-provider call was made because real credentials must not be used without explicit permission.
No deployed Cloudflare/NUC browser session was exercised; verification is application, jsdom, and disposable-PostgreSQL evidence.
The full cross-device/manual accessibility matrix remains a release-level validation task outside this slice.
sourceHashNote: The tutor route/UI hashes reflect the final follow-up revisions proven by the two linked reports; the original lifecycle-only checks remain represented by the unchanged lifecycle service, thread routes, and PostgreSQL fixture hashes.
